title: urun
html:
urun

Run a Uniface form, similar to using the deprecated run ProcScript statement.

Important: The urun function is deprecated and should not be used. Instead you use the Call-In API, a collection of call-in functions related to the ProcScript activate command. For more information, see the "Call-In Using the Call-In API" topic.
long urun (unsigned char*Form, intVpos, intHpos, intVsize, intHsize);

Parameters

Form
Name of the form to be executed, in uppercase or lowercase. It makes a difference whether you use the .frm suffix, as described below.
Vpos
Horizontal position of the form (Character mode only)
Hpos
Vertical position of the form (Character mode only)
Vsize
Height of the form (Character mode only)
Hsize
Width of the form (Character mode only)

Return Values

Under normal working conditions, the urun function returns the value returned by the exec operation if it contains a return or exit statement. If these are not encountered, the following values can be returned:

Table 1. Values returned by urun
Value Meaning
10 The user left the form with ^QUIT
9 The user left the form with ^ACCEPT
0 The form did not contain an edit or display statement in the exec operation.
-1 The form could not be found.
-3 The form is non-modal and, therefore, cannot be executed with urun .

If the form name does not contain the .frm suffix, and something fails when starting the form, urun can return error codes related to the activate command.

Use

Library: yrtl

Description

urun is used to start a Uniface form. If your main application is a 3GL application, it is required that you have successfully called unifbeg before you call urun. Once a form has started, Uniface has control until the form ends execution. Control then returns to the 3GL program or module.

Note: urun uses the activate ProcScript command internally to run the form.
  • You must have a compiled signature for the form available, for example, in your UAR file.
  • The parameters for form position and size are ignored.
  • urun can return a variety of error codes related to activate, as described in the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.
  • urun sets the value of $SEARCH_DESCRIPTOR to DBMS_FIRST, even if you specify differently in your assignment file.

To make urun behave the way it did in Uniface V7 and earlier in a character-mode screen, specify the form name with the .frm suffix, for example:

urun("ent.frm", 2, 2, 70, 20);

The Vpos and Hpos parameters are used to position the top left corner of the form in the character-mode screen. The top left-hand corner of a screen is at position (0,0). Similarly, the Vsize and Hsize parameters can be used to alter the height and width of the form. All these parameters have no effect in a GUI application. If Vpos, Hpos, Vsize, and Hsize are zero, the default values defined for the form are used.

The following character mode call-in program starts the Uniface environment and runs two different forms of different sizes, one after the another, positioning them at different places on the screen. This has the visual effect of two forms on one screen, although of course only one is active at any one given time. This program was tested on Sun Solaris.

#include <h3gl.h>

#define ENT1 (unsigned char*)"ENT1.frm"
#define ENT2 (unsigned char*)"ENT2.frm"

void main ()
{
(void)unifbeg(0); /* Initialize Uniface in interactive mode */

(void)urun(ENT1, 2, 10, 0, 0); /* Run ENT1 on row 2, col 10 */
(void)urun(ENT2, 12, 30, 0, 0); /* Run ENT2 on row 12, col 30 */

(void)unifend(-1); /* Shutdown and terminate program */
}
Related Links
  • Call-In Using the Call-In API

    The Uniface 3GL Call-in API is a collection of call-in functions related to the ProcScript activate command.

  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.
